summary: Both major event-signature deviations from round 1 are correctly fixed. The @return NatSpec addition is good. The two new additions (ReentrancyGuard slot comment and treasury-DOS operator note) are well-intentioned but the ReentrancyGuard comment contains a factual inaccuracy about OZ 5.x constants that should be corrected to avoid misleading future auditors. The treasury-DOS note is valuable and correctly placed. No blocking issues remain.

T4-C-01.description: VaultDeployed.vault is no longer indexed — matches tech-spec Data Models line 302 exactly.

T4-C-02.description: PlatformTreasuryUpdated args are no longer indexed — matches tech-spec Data Models line 304 exactly.

T4-C-05.description: @return vault NatSpec added to register() — clear and correct.

T4-C-03.description: Reviewer self-marked no bug, skip is appropriate.

T4-C-04.description: Task spec implementation hints explicitly use this variable name. Skip is justified.

new_issues.title: ReentrancyGuard slot comment contains factual imprecision about NOT_ENTERED constant
new_issues.description: The added comment states: 'OZ 5.x checks `_status == ENTERED (2)`, so the zero-default is safe for the first withdraw and the guard transitions through ENTERED→NOT_ENTERED on use.' This is behaviorally correct — a clone starting with `_status = 0` will pass the `_status == ENTERED` check (0 != 2) — but it omits a critical fact: `NOT_ENTERED = 1`, not 0. The actual OZ 5.x constants are `NOT_ENTERED = 1` and `ENTERED = 2` (verified in `lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/utils/ReentrancyGuard.sol` lines 34-35). The clone starts at 0 (uninitialized), which works because the guard only rejects `ENTERED (2)`, not 0. After the first successful `nonReentrant` call, `_status` becomes `NOT_ENTERED (1)` — not 0 — and remains at 1 thereafter. A future auditor reading this comment may falsely conclude that `_status` permanently stays at 0 or that `NOT_ENTERED == 0`, leading to incorrect reasoning about guard state. Additionally: the correct place to explain this EIP-1167 + ReentrancyGuard interaction is the constructor NatSpec or contract-level docs, not inside `initialize`, since the concern is about the guard slot not being set by the constructor, which has nothing to do with initialization logic.

new_issues.title: Treasury-DOS operator note is accurate and correctly placed
new_issues.description: The added NatSpec documents the known risk that a reverting `platformTreasury` (misconfigured contract address) freezes withdrawals when `fee > 0`. The description is accurate: developer funds are not lost, just inaccessible until the factory owner calls `setPlatformTreasury` with a working address. Developer-first transfer ordering (T4 systemic-fix §7) means the developer always receives their `net` portion before the treasury transfer is attempted, so partial DOS (only the fee transfer failing) is the actual failure mode — the comment is slightly imprecise on this. However the overall guidance ('treasury MUST be a plain EOA or audited multisig') is correct and useful for operators. No change required.

required_changes: Fix T4-R2-01: Correct the ReentrancyGuard slot comment — NOT_ENTERED is 1 not 0; move it to constructor NatSpec where it contextually belongs.
positive_observations: All round 1 required changes applied correctly and verified against tech-spec.
Treasury-DOS operator note is a good proactive addition that documents a real operational risk.
The @return vault NatSpec is clean and accurate.
overall_assessment: Implementation is correct and spec-compliant after round 1 fixes. The only outstanding item is a minor factual imprecision in a non-functional comment (T4-R2-01). If contracts-author corrects the ReentrancyGuard comment, this task is ready to close. If the comment correction is deferred, the code itself remains functionally correct — the guard works properly regardless of the comment wording.
